growth_records.cite: Zhu et al. Systematic modulation of bacterial resource allocation by perturbing RNA polymerase availability via synthetic transcriptional switches. Nucleic Acids Research (2025). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaf814. [PMC12370630]
growth_records.snippet: The modulations of growth rates spanned from zero growth to maximal saturated growth rates, reaching ∼1.85 h−1 in LB medium and ∼1.4 h−1 in glucose + cAA medium, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Maximal (saturated-IPTG, WT-equivalent) exponential growth rate directly reported in text for B. subtilis 168-derived strains in LB medium. Growth curves obtained from OD600 measurements (0.05-0.5 range) in an air-bath shaker at 200 rpm, 37°C (Materials and methods, 'Cell culturing procedure'). No calculation performed, value taken directly as stated (∼1.85 h^-1).

growth_records.strain: strain 168 (trpC2); SigA- and RpoBC-titratable (P_gracO2 promoter-replacement) derivatives at saturating IPTG (30 µM), growth rate equivalent to wild-type
growth_records.cite: Zhu et al. Systematic modulation of bacterial resource allocation by perturbing RNA polymerase availability via synthetic transcriptional switches. Nucleic Acids Research (2025). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaf814. [PMC12370630]
growth_records.snippet: reaching ∼1.85 h−1 in LB medium and ∼1.4 h−1 in glucose + cAA medium, respectively. At an IPTG level of 30 µM, the growth rates of both titratable strains had reached saturation... being equivalent to those of wild-type
growth_records.notes: Maximal (saturated-IPTG, WT-equivalent) exponential growth rate directly reported in text for B. subtilis 168-derived strains in glucose+cAA (modified C-minimal) medium. Same value repeated later in Results as 'high' condition (1.4 h^-1; SigA_high and RpoBC_high) 'comparable to the growth rate of wild-type strain'. No calculation performed.

growth_records.strain: strain 168 derivatives, SigA-titratable (P_gracO2-sigA) and RpoBC-titratable (P_gracO2-rpoBC), at intermediate (sub-saturating) IPTG level - partial SigA or RpoBC depletion
growth_records.cite: Zhu et al. Systematic modulation of bacterial resource allocation by perturbing RNA polymerase availability via synthetic transcriptional switches. Nucleic Acids Research (2025). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaf814. [PMC12370630]
growth_records.snippet: a condition of intermediate growth rate (0.6–0.8 h−1; SigA_medium, RpoBC_medium)
growth_records.notes: Paper explicitly reports an intermediate growth-rate condition of 0.6-0.8 h^-1 for partially SigA- or RpoBC-depleted strain 168 derivatives (sub-saturating IPTG) in glucose+cAA medium; growth_rate_per_h field set to the midpoint (0.7 h^-1) of the reported range - see reported range in this note. This is a genetically-perturbed (RNAP-limited) growth condition, not unmodified wild-type growth.

growth_records.cite: Seffouh et al. YsxC is a placeholder for ribosomal protein uL2 during 50S ribosomal subunit assembly. Nucleic Acids Research (2025). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaf1071. [PMC12560758]
growth_records.snippet: incubated at 37°C with agitation until it reached a doubling time of 120 min, which usually corresponds to A600 0.3–0.4. ... Doubling times of cultures were calculated as DT = (t2 − t1) × [ln2/(ln OD600@t2/ln OD600@t1)].
growth_records.notes: B. subtilis YsxC-depletion strain RB260 grown in LB + 5 µg/ml chloramphenicol at 37°C; doubling time of 120 min (2 h) explicitly reported (calculated by authors from OD600 time-course via DT formula given in Methods). growth_rate_per_h calculated by us as mu=ln2/DT=ln2/2=0.347 h-1 from the paper's reported 120 min doubling time.

growth_records.strain: Δ6 (reference strain, progenitor of genome-reduced lineage)
growth_records.cite: Reuß et al. Large-scale reduction of the Bacillus subtilis genome: consequences for the transcriptional network, resource allocation, and metabolism. Genome Research (2017). DOI:10.1101/gr.215293.116. [PMC5287234]
growth_records.snippet: Growth of the bacteria was analyzed in complex medium supplemented with glucose. The reference strain had a generation time of 21.5 ± 2.3 min, whereas the deletion strains PG10 and PS38 doubled every 33.0 ± 2.2 and 29.3
growth_records.notes: Doubling time reported as 21.5 ± 2.3 min (=0.3583 h) for reference strain Δ6. mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/0.3583 = 1.93 h^-1. Base LB recipe not numerically specified in this paper (only the 0.5% glucose supplement is quantified); oxygenation status not explicitly stated in text but standard shake-flask B. subtilis cultivation is aerobic. Growth measured during physiological characterization in bioreactor/flask culture, same conditions later used for multi-omics sampling (preculture to OD600 0.5-0.8, diluted to OD600 0.05 in LB+0.5% glucose at 37°C, grown to OD600 0.5).

growth_records.cite: Reuß et al. Large-scale reduction of the Bacillus subtilis genome: consequences for the transcriptional network, resource allocation, and metabolism. Genome Research (2017). DOI:10.1101/gr.215293.116. [PMC5287234]
growth_records.snippet: Growth of the bacteria was analyzed in complex medium supplemented with glucose... the deletion strains PG10 and PS38 doubled every 33.0 ± 2.2 and 29.3 ± 0.4 min, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time reported as 33.0 ± 2.2 min (=0.55 h) for genome-reduced strain PG10 (88 deletions, ~36% genome removed vs B. subtilis 168). mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/0.55 = 1.26 h^-1. Same medium/temperature as reference strain Δ6 record in this batch; oxygenation assumed aerobic (standard B. subtilis shake culture, not explicitly stated).

growth_records.cite: Reuß et al. Large-scale reduction of the Bacillus subtilis genome: consequences for the transcriptional network, resource allocation, and metabolism. Genome Research (2017). DOI:10.1101/gr.215293.116. [PMC5287234]
growth_records.snippet: Growth of the bacteria was analyzed in complex medium supplemented with glucose... the deletion strains PG10 and PS38 doubled every 33.0 ± 2.2 and 29.3 ± 0.4 min, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time reported as 29.3 ± 0.4 min (=0.4883 h) for genome-reduced strain PS38 (94 deletions, ~36% genome removed vs B. subtilis 168). mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/0.4883 = 1.42 h^-1. Same medium/temperature as reference strain Δ6 record in this batch; oxygenation assumed aerobic (standard B. subtilis shake culture, not explicitly stated). Note: genome-reduced strains also showed strongly reduced pyruvate secretion vs Δ6 (Supplemental Table S4, not quantified numerically in main text), so no secretion rate could be extracted.

growth_records.cite: Cheng et al. Programming bacteria for multiplexed DNA detection. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-37582-x. [PMC10086068]
growth_records.snippet: The inferred slope of the linear function is determined by the cell doubling time (~0.5 h) and intercept is determined by the background mutation frequency (Fig. 2d–g).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (~0.5 h) for the transformed, GFP-expressing B. subtilis sub-population is stated directly in the text, but it is itself an approximate value the authors inferred from the slope of a linear fit to log[gDNA] vs. detection-time data (not a direct OD/CFU growth curve), hence low confidence. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/0.5 h = 1.386 /h. Medium/temperature taken from the Methods description of the liquid-culture fluorescence detection-time assay (LB + IPTG + chloramphenicol + MLS, 37°C shaking) that generated Fig. 2d-g.

growth_records.cite: van Gestel et al. From Cell Differentiation to Cell Collectives: Bacillus subtilis Uses Division of Labor to Migrate. PLoS Biology (2015). DOI:10.1371/journal.pbio.1002141. [PMC4403855]
growth_records.snippet: cells were grown in 5-ml LB broth cultures for eight consecutive cycles...At the onset of each new cycle, the cells were diluted to a target OD600 of 0.004...On average, cells divided 4.12 ± 0.36 times per cycle, making

growth_records.cite: Wang, Song et al. Metabolic engineering of Bacillus subtilis for the production of active hemoglobins and myoglobins by improving heme supply. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (PNAS) (2025). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2504795122. [PMC12260557]
growth_records.snippet: The highest titers of Hb and Mb were obtained at 15 h of fed-batch fermentation. The titers of S-Hb, C-Hb, B-Mb, and P-Mb reached 0.81, 0.82, 1.11, and 1.01 g L-1, equivalent to the productivities of 54.00, 54.67, 73.33, and 67.33 mg L-1 h-
growth_records.notes: Metabolic-engineering / recombinant-protein production paper; the only quantitative experimental rates are VOLUMETRIC product productivities of recombinant hemoproteins in a 5 L fed-batch (BSM medium, gluconate-induced), NOT metabolic exchange fluxes and NOT biomass-normalized (no gDW). No specific growth rate (mu), no substrate uptake rate, no O2 uptake, no Yxs, and no MFA are reported (full methods and BSM base composition are in an unavailable SI Appendix). Arithmetic check of paper's productivities from titer/time: 0.81 g/L=810 mg/L / 15 h = 54.0 mg/L/h; 0.82 g/L=820/15=54.67; 1.11 g/L~1100/15=73.33; 1.01 g/L=1010/15=67.33 (all match reported values). Feed additives stated: sodium gluconate 40 g/L (inducer), ammonium sulfate 24 g/L, yeast extract 48 g/L. Temperature/pH/aeration not given in main text. A separate 5 L fed-batch of strain Heme-K2 gave a heme titer of 282.17 mg/L on day 6 (no per-hour productivity stated). Confidence low for GEM-constraint use because rates are volumetric protein productivities, not gDW-normalized metabolite exchange fluxes.
